channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Wheel falls off Link Airways flight during take-off from Canberra Airport
item.description: Damage to bearing parts meant investigators could not determine what caused a wheel to separate from a Link Airways-operated flight departing Canberra for Newcastle.
item.dc:creator: Lois Maskiell

media:group.media:description: The left inboard main landing gear wheel separated from a Saab 340 during take-off.

item.title: Man charged over alleged hit and run crash after 13yo boy critically injured
item.description: The young cyclist was riding at dusk when he was hit by a ute which allegedly failed to stop and caused him to suffer critical leg injuries.
item.dc:creator: Josh Dye

media:group.media:description: Police have charged the driver of a 4WD dual cab utility after the alleged hit and run crash.

item.title: ISIS-linked woman 'scared to go outside' after Sydney return, father says
item.description: The father of one of the returned ISIS-linked women says his daughter is "happy to be home" but exhausted after more than a decade stranded in Syria.
item.dc:creator: Danuta Kozaki

media:group.media:description: Nesrine Zahab is one of the ISIS-linked women who have returned to Australia from Syria.

item.title: Hopes inquest into Hillcrest jumping castle tragedy will begin in September
item.description: A coronial inquest into the deaths of six children at a Tasmanian primary school could begin in September, nearly five years after the tragedy occurred.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie Heard

media:group.media:description: Six children died in the jumping castle incident. From top left: Chace Harrison, Jalailah Jayne-Maree Jones, Zane Mellor. From bottom left: Addison Stewart, Jye Sheehan, Peter Dodt.

item.title: Corruption watchdog criticises three-year police integrity unit delay
item.description: Crime and Corruption Commission chair Bruce Barbour has told a parliamentary inquiry the Queensland Police Service has systemic issues with handling complaints.
item.dc:creator: Alex Brewster

media:group.media:description: The Crime and Corruption Commission has criticised a three-year delay in the establishment of a police integrity unit.

item.title: Greens signal willingness to help Labor push capital gains changes through in weeks
item.description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers introduced the bill to parliament today, which the government hopes to pass before the winter recess.
item.dc:creator: Tom Crowley

media:group.media:description: Jim Chalmers introduced the capital gains tax bill to the lower house today.

item.title: 'Really the wrong thing': Solomon refuses to consider full-time Dons gig
item.description: Despite being Brad Scott's immediate replacement, Essendon interim coach Dean Solomon is not throwing up his hands for the full-time role any time soon.
item.dc:creator: Chris De Silva

media:group.media:description: Dean Solomon says he is currently not interested in chasing Essendon's full-time coaching job.

item.title: Paedophile Ashley Paul Griffith argues life sentence manifestly excessive
item.description: The former childcare worker pleaded guilty in 2024 to more than 300 charges and was handed a life sentence with a non-parole period of 27 years.
item.dc:creator: Talissa Siganto

media:group.media:description: Ashley Paul Griffith pleaded guilty to hundreds of child sex offences from his time working at childcare centres.

item.title: Shopping habits to help reduce your grocery spend
item.description: Small changes to the way we approach our meal planning can make a big difference to the overall cost.
item.dc:creator: Amy Sheehan

media:group.media:description: Simple steps like having a list and making fewer visits to the supermarket can help save you money.

item.title: $30k fine for man who distributed thousands of pamphlets targeting MP
item.description: A finance executive has been fined $30,000 over unauthorised pamphlets that targeted the Wentworth MP in the lead up to the federal election.
item.dc:creator: Jamie McKinnell

media:group.media:description: The Australian Electoral Commission said over 47,000 unauthorised leaflets attacking independent MP Allegra Spender circulated in her electorate of Wentworth.

item.title: Meet the Iranians who gather every night outside Khamanei's house
item.description: Outside the Tehran compound that was home to now-dead Iranian supreme leader Ali Khamenei, hundreds of his supporters gather every night to mourn the only leader many of them have ever known.
item.dc:creator: Karishma Vyas

media:group.media:description: Mourners upset over Ali Khamenei's death crowd in front of his house.

item.title: US Justice Department launches criminal probe into E Jean Carroll
item.description: The probe is focused on whether Carroll committed perjury in testimony tied to two civil lawsuits that she won against Mr Trump — one tied to ‌her allegations that he sexually abused her in a New York department store.

media:group.media:description: A 2023 jury found Donald Trump sexually abused E Jean Carroll in a civil case.

item.title: Canberra man becomes first person in ACT charged with exit trafficking
item.description: The man, who left his wife in India and did not return for the birth of his son, is the first person in the ACT to be charged with the crime.
item.dc:creator: Zaarkacha Marlan and Tahlia Roy

media:group.media:description: A Canberra man has become the first person in the ACT to be charged with exit trafficking.

item.title: Housing investors are increasingly older and wealthier, Reserve Bank research shows
item.description: Reserve Bank research shows property investment has become skewed towards higher-income households and is dominated by baby boomers.
item.dc:creator: Gareth Hutchens

media:group.media:description: Reserve Bank research shows housing investors have become increasingly older and wealthier in the past 20 years.

item.title: 15yo Indian phenom smashes Cummins to set new IPL sixes record
item.description: Australia's captain feels the brunt of the pain as Indian sensational Vaibhav Sooryavanshi continues his phenomenal IPL form.

media:group.media:description: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi (pictured) continued his remarkable IPL season by laying waste to Pat Cummins's Hyderabad side.

item.title: Controversial CIT CEO gives emphatic denial of EV training misconduct allegations
item.description: The CEO of the Canberra Institute of Technology has strongly denied allegations that student mechanics worked on Hertz hire cars without the company's knowledge, insisting the claims are "factually incorrect" and "offensive" to staff and students.
item.dc:creator: Lois Maskiell

media:group.media:description: Margot McNeill was appointed to the position of CIT chief executive in June last year.

item.title: Emergency hay deliveries resume for WA stations after 'generous' donations
item.description: Emergency hay deliveries to drought-stricken areas resume after thousands of dollars in donations from school students and charity groups.
item.dc:creator: Georgia Hargreaves, Alistair Bates, Tara de Landgrafft, and Joanna Prendergast

media:group.media:description: Hay bales from farms on WA's South coast are being loaded onto trucks bound for drought stricken stations in the state's north.

item.title: Council to demolish in-demand childcare centre for open space
item.description: A childcare centre's director says relocation had been promised for years until late last year, when the council decided there was no feasible site in the area.
item.dc:creator: Declan Bowring

media:group.media:description: At least 40 children will need to find new childcare arrangements if the centre closes at the end of this year.

item.title: Calls for a higher bar for developers to protect significant Cairns shade trees
item.description: After several failed court challenges, a council has angered a community with its approval to remove a significant tree to make way for a duplex development, but experts say local governments need to start paying more attention to residents' concerns.
item.dc:creator: Conor Byrne and Christopher Testa

media:group.media:description: The visit of Gardening Australia host Costa Georgadis to the Freshwater raintree in February raised the profile of the tree's plight to a national level.

item.title: Four EzyMart Sydney stores shut over illegal tobacco, vape sale claims
item.description: The major Australian retail company will part ways with an independent operator after several Sydney stores were shut down for allegedly selling illegal tobacco and vapes.
item.dc:creator: Sean Tarek Goodwin

media:group.media:description: NSW Health enforcement officers issued four EzyMart convenience shops in Sydney with 90-day closure orders.

item.title: Government sues 3M Australia for $2 billion over PFAS contamination
item.description: The Commonwealth is launching its largest-ever legal claim, suing the manufacturer of a firefighting foam containing PFAS chemicals that contaminated 28 Defence Force bases across Australia.
item.dc:creator: Xanthe Gregory

media:group.media:description: Firefighting foam manufacturer 3M says it will defend itself against the Australian government's damages claim.

item.title: 'Unsafe and late': What it's like catching a bus in this regional city
item.description: Amid a spate of assaults at bus stops in Far North Queensland, advocates say there's a need for change.
item.dc:creator: Rosanne Maloney

media:group.media:description: Questions have arisen about safety after one man was allegedly stabbed and two others assaulted at a Cairns bus stop.

item.title: Woman who returned to Australia last year charged with joining IS
item.description: Police conducted searches at homes in Melbourne's north early on Thursday as part of ongoing investigations into people who have returned from Syria.
item.dc:creator: Max Walden and James Oaten

media:group.media:description: The ABC understands the woman is Rayann El Houli.

item.title: Search for swimmer resumes at Bunbury, police call for information
item.description: Water police search for a swimmer who was reported to be in distress at Koombana Bay in Bunbury on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Stan Shaw and Gabrielle Becerra Mellet

media:group.media:description: The search for the woman has resumed.

item.title: 'Remarkable' large funnel cloud spotted in regional Victoria
item.description: The Bureau of Meteorology says it is unusual to see large funnel clouds so far west in Victoria.
item.dc:creator: Laura Mayers and Stephen Martin

media:group.media:description: The large funnel was spotted yesterday near Mount Hooghly in north-west Victoria.
